action_text: Inspect the crossing before the peak; record scour, debris and abutment condition.
Deploy an inspection team; stage closure signage and barriers at both approaches.
Warn transporters and boda stages that this crossing is expected to close.
Close the crossing, man both approaches, and log the closure time.
Broadcast the closure by radio and WhatsApp in Lumasaba and English (DRAFT, human-approved).
Barricade the cut section and post it on the district road-status board.
Send the pre-agreed local-language alert to the chief by radio and WhatsApp; state plainly that no road alternate exists.
Confirm boat or foot-crossing capacity and a stretcher-referral plan with the village health team.
Pre-position a 72-hour food and safe-water reserve on the settlement's own side of the crossing.
Publish the longer route and its travel time to the chief, boda stages and the ambulance service.
Warn floodplain households; name the assembly point and the person who calls the move.
Pre-position the essential drug kit and delivery supplies on the far side of the crossing.
Agree the referral route and standby transport with the sub-county ambulance before the crossing closes.
Lift drugs, records and the generator above the expected flood level.
Confirm the school can serve as the assembly point: water, latrines, roof.
Lift records and desks above the expected flood level; suspend classes if severity rises.
Test and chlorinate the alternate source serving the cut-off side.
Mark the source contaminated once floodwater reaches it and announce the substitute source.
